Article Title: 'Similar insensitivity on full display after one year': Congress on Dhankhar resignation
Article Text:
Congress on Dhankhar resignation (ANI image)

NEW DELHI: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh on Tuesday criticised the Centre on the first anniversary of former Vice President Jagdeep Dhankhar's resignation, alleging that he was forced to step down three years into his five-year tenure because he repeatedly spoke about the plight of farmers.In a post on X, Ramesh said Dhankhar was appointed "with great fanfare" by Prime Minister Narendra Modi but was abruptly made to resign after expressing anguish over farmers' distress and what he described as the government's "shocking insensitivity" towards them.The Congress leader said the same "insensitivity and indifference" was now visible in the government's handling of the concerns of lakhs of students and youth, referring to ongoing protests over alleged examination irregularities and demands for greater transparency in the education system."It was exactly a year ago today that the then Vice President of India and Chairman of the Rajya Sabha was forcibly made to resign just three years into his five-year tenure. He was appointed with great fanfare by the Prime Minister but was suddenly made to exit because he repeatedly expressed anguish at the plight and distress of farmers and the Modi Govt's shocking insensitivity towards them — a similar insensitivity and indifference now on full display in regard to the pressing concerns of lakhs of students and youth," Ramesh wrote.Ramesh also alleged that Dhankhar was not even given the courtesy of a formal farewell by members of the Rajya Sabha after demitting office.Jagdeep Dhankhar assumed office as Vice President in August 2022 and was due to complete his five-year term in 2027. However, on the opening day of the Monsoon Session of Parliament on July 21 last year, he unexpectedly resigned, citing health reasons.In his resignation letter to President Droupadi Murmu, Dhankhar wrote, "To prioritise health care and abide by medical advice, I hereby resign as Vice-President of India, effective immediately, in accordance with Article 67(a) of the Constitution."President Murmu subsequently accepted his resignation. Dhankhar became only the third Vice President after V V Giri and R Venkataraman to step down before completing a full term. Both Giri and Venkataraman had resigned to contest the presidential election.
Article Summary: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh criticised the Centre on the first anniversary of former Vice President Jagdeep Dhankhar's resignation. He alleged that he was forced to step down three years into his five-year tenure because he repeatedly spoke about the plight of farmers. The Congress leader said the same "insensitivity and indifference" was now visible in the handling of the concerns of lakhs of students and youth.

Article Title: India summons Russian diplomat over 4 Indians killed in strike off Ukrainian coast
Article Text:
Four Indian nationals were killed after a commercial vessel, MV Golden Leo, came under attac.

NEW DELHI: India on Tuesday summoned Russian diplomat over the killing of four Indians on board a merchant vessel off the coast of Ukraine, reported news agency PTI citing sources.Four Indian nationals were killed after a commercial vessel, MV Golden Leo, came under attack while departing Ukraine's port of Odesa on Sunday, the Indian government confirmed. One more Indian crew member remains in critical condition.According to the government, the vessel had 17 crew members on board, including five Indian nationals, when it was attacked.India strongly condemned the strike, saying attacks on commercial shipping and civilian crew members were unacceptable.India, in a statement, said, “As per the information available, four Indian nationals have tragically lost their lives, while one is hospitalised in a critical condition. Our mission in Ukraine is closely monitoring the situation and is making every effort to extend all possible assistance to those affected.”The government also reiterated that targeting commercial vessels, endangering civilian seafarers and disrupting freedom of navigation and international trade was deplorable and should be avoided.One of those killed has been identified as 26-year-old merchant navy officer Akhil Joyan from Vellarikkundu in Kerala's Kasaragod district. His family was informed of his death by the shipping company through an email on Monday.Akhil had recently returned home on leave, got engaged and had resumed duty only a month ago.According to a Reuters report, Ukrainian officials said a Russian missile strike hit a ship carrying corn near the southern port of Odesa, killing 10 people.
Article Summary: Four Indian nationals were killed after a commercial vessel, MV Golden Leo, came under attack while departing Ukraine's port of Odesa on Sunday. One more Indian crew member remains in critical condition. India strongly condemned the strike, saying attacks on commercial shipping and civilian crew members were unacceptable.

Article Title: Indus Water Treaty in present form no longer exists, Pak aiding terrorism: India
Article Text: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan will no longer function in its present form, the government has concluded hardening its position on the contentious water issue amid little indication of Islamabad discontinuing its covert backing of cross-border terrorism.India is not yet actively considering abandoning the World Bank-brokered 1960 Treaty – an option it still has on the table - but a source speaking on condition of anonymity said it's not in India's interest to remain a party to the Treaty anymore.India had formally sought a review and modification of the IWT citing demographic shifts, clean energy priorities and new climate realities, prior to putting the Treaty in "abeyance" last year in retaliation for the Pahalgam terrorist attack."It would be accurate to say that the IWT in its present form will not function at all. If provisions related to river water sharing have to be reactivated, they will have to be done in a different form. Not in the present form," said the source, adding India will await further developments before considering the maximalist option of withdrawing altogether from the IWT.The IWT does not have an exit clause, but it's of significance to India that Article 60 of the Vienna Convention on the Law of Treaties (VCLT) states that a significant violation of a treaty by one party allows other affected parties to suspend the treaty in whole or in part.While India and Pakistan are both not party to the VCLT, India believes that if it invoked customary international law, then Pakistan's state sponsorship of terrorism could be considered such a breach of obligations.According to Indian authorities, as a sovereign state, India cannot be compelled to stay in a treaty that is demonstrably not in its interest. These assertions come in the middle of efforts by Pakistan to internationalise the IWT issue by citing "unilateral and unlawful" suspension of the Treaty and claiming it threatens the water security and livelihoods of its 240 million citizens. Outside of the Treaty framework, India continues to provide issue flood warnings to Pakistan to ensure protection of lives and property.India believes that Pakistan has created an elaborate victimhood narrative, ignoring the fact that more water of the Indus system is lost in Pakistan due to surface runoff than India's entire share under the Indus water stream. Sources also point to how Pakistan has since 1976 not built a single large-scale storage project or invested in modern water management, despite receiving nearly 80% of the Indus Basin waters under the Treaty. As far as India is concerned, Pakistan's problem is water management, and not water availability.Fow India though, the central issue remains Pakistan's support to cross-border terrorism. India maintains that there is no deadline for keeping the IWT in abeyance. "The Treaty will remain in abeyance until Pakistan credibly and irrevocably abjures its support for cross-border terrorism. This is the threshold condition," said a source, adding that Pakistan continues to provide enabling platforms just across the LoC to terrorist groups and individuals banned by the UNSC 1267 Sanctions Committee."During this period, India will pursue the optimal utilisation of its legitimate rights through scientific basin management, modern engineering practices, infrastructure modernisation and responsible stewardship of water resources," said the source.
Article Summary: India had formally sought a review and modification of the IWT citing demographic shifts, clean energy priorities and new climate realities. India put the Treaty in "abeyance" last year in retaliation for the Pahalgam terrorist attack. India believes that Pakistan has created an elaborate victimhood narrative, ignoring the fact that more water of the Indus system is lost in Pakistan.

Article Title: Supreme Court takes exception to meagre compensation for ex-HC judges
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Supreme Court Monday took exception to the meagre monetary compensation provided to retired chief justices and judges of high courts, which varies vastly from one state to another, and asked the Union govt to constitute a committee to lay down uniform pan-India guidelines.A bench of CJI Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and V Mohana took exception to the UP govt’s challenge to an interim order of Allahabad HC directing the state to provide security even though it is paying Rs 65,000 and Rs 60,000 to retired CJs and judges of the HC towards security, a driver, domestic help, secretarial services, and telephone and internet expenses.The bench said HC judges, while holding constitutional posts, decide sensitive matters and their apprehensions about personal security are genuine. “Post-retirement, the judges need basic minimum facilities to lead a dignified life. Can Rs 60,000 provide all these services? We all know how much one needs to pay to employ a driver and a domestic help,” the CJI-led bench said.Finding solicitor general Tushar Mehta in the courtroom, the bench asked him to impress upon Centre to constitute a committee in two weeks to consider what should be the basic minimum security and services that every state must provide to retired HC CJs and judges to enable them to lead a dignified life.“We are not talking of a luxurious life, but a dignified life,” the CJI said.Mehta said such a panel would be constituted within two weeks as per request of the Supreme Court. The bench said the committee will study the issues faced by retired HC judges and give its recommendations within three months laying down basic guidelines on the obligations of the states to extend facilities to judges post-retirement.
Article Summary: Supreme Court asks Union govt to constitute a committee to lay down uniform pan- India guidelines. The bench said HC judges, while holding constitutional posts, decide sensitive matters and their apprehensions about personal security are genuine. “We are not talking of a luxurious life, but a dignified life,” the CJI said.

Article Title: Youth full of aspirations, want country to move forward: PM Modi
Article Text:
Prime Minister Narendra Modi addresses the media ahead of the start of the Monsoon Session of Parliament in New Delhi on Monday. (AP image)

NEW DELHI: On a day when the Cockroach Janta Party-headed protest against govt over NEET-UG paper leak and myriad issues turned violent, PM Narendra Modi Monday said the Indian youth is full of aspirations and want the country to move forward and, in a message to opposition parties, called for using logic and facts to raise issues in Parliament instead of causing disruption.In his customary remarks to media before the start of Parliament’s monsoon session, he said the country has notched up several achievements in the past one month and brought up the debut success of a private space company — Skyroot — in launching a rocket into the orbit, noting that the average age of its team is only 28 years. “I am not speaking of a 56-year-old young man. I am talking about a start-up whose team, with an average age of just 28, has planted India’s flag in space,” Modi said in an apparent swipe at leader of opposition Rahul Gandhi, who is 56. PM said the successful launch has given the message that capabilities and aspirations of India’s youth are as limitless as space itself. Reforms pursued by his govt have enabled the youth to take such bold steps, he added.Calling for a productive session, he said it is the need of the hour that Parliament give voice to people filled with patriotism. “...If logic and facts are strong, then one can calmly raise an issue in an emphatic manner.”“Be it the monsoon or the monsoon session, when both are proactive, they become highly productive. And when both are productive, they contribute to the well-being of the nation.” He spoke of the recent inaugurations of an oil refinery and the country’s third semiconductor plant besides the launch of a hydrogen-powered train as among the recent feats. He said the country has picked up speed, and Parliament’s spirit can infuse new energy into this momentum.Referring to the West Asia conflict, Modi said the war has posed a major crisis for countries like India which are dependent on others for energy. He said petrol, diesel, LPG, fertilisers, chemicals — all faced disruptions, yet, India continued to grow at 7.7%, remaining the fastest-growing major economy in the world.
Article Summary: PM Narendra Modi said the Indian youth is full of aspirations and want the country to move forward. In a message to opposition parties, he called for using logic and facts to raise issues in Parliament instead of causing disruption. He said the country has notched up several achievements in the past one month.

Article Title: Can't force parents to create Apaar IDs for students: Supreme Court
Article Text: New Delhi: In an order that would apply to 24 crore students, Supreme Court on Monday said parents cannot be mandated to create 'one student, one unique ID' for their children under Automated Permanent Academic Account Registry (Apaar) scheme, even as it noted the benefits of the scheme.Appearing for three parents, whose children are studying in various grades in schools, senior advocate Indira Jaising said for creation of Apaar ID, schools are mandating the parents to provide Aadhaar number of their children, thus obliging them to create Aadhaar IDs for their children, even though SC in K S Puttaswamy case had said it would be optional. Moreover, the data pertaining to children stored through Apaar would endure through their lives. "No one, in their mid-careers, would want the public to know how they fared academically in their school days. It is purely a question of right to privacy ," she said and claimed that Digital Personal Data Protection Act provisions have not been applied to Apaar scheme.In addition, right to education is a fundamental right and not a service provided by govt. "For providing a service, the SC's 9-J bench had ruled that Aadhaar cannot be insisted upon for establishing one's identity," she said.She pointed out that Orissa high court had recently taken exception to parents being asked for consent for Apaar as it did not provide an 'opt out' option to parents and had directed state govt to consider amending the consent forms and providing such an option.The CJI-led bench asked whether the HC judgment had been challenged before SC. Jaising replied in the negative and said it has attained finality. SC said it would refer to the HC order and pass a ruling along similar lines which were enforceable pan-India.Before conveying its intended decision, the bench said everything need not be seen through the lens of suspicion. Creation of Apaar ID would help students shift from one school to another as transmission of their details can happen instantaneously, a task which used to take months with the tedious process of obtaining transfer certificates followed by physical transfer of records.The petitioners said, "The architecture of Apaar, involving centralised storage and updating of students' personal and academic data, effectively operates as a State-run surveillance infrastructure in the education sector."
Article Summary: Supreme Court said parents cannot be mandated to create 'one student, one unique ID' for their children under Automated Permanent Academic Account Registry (Apaar) scheme. Appearing for three parents, senior advocate Indira Jaising said for creation of Apaar ID, schools are mandating the parents to provide Aadhaar number of their children.

Article Title: Delhi violence not spontaneous, says BJP, blames Left
Article Text: NEW DELHI: BJP Monday blamed Left-affiliated student organisations, such as All India Students’ Association (AISA) and Students’ Federation of India (SFI), besides organised groups for protests and violence during Cockroach Janata Party’s march to Parliament.Similar allegations of organised instigation and crowd mobilisation were made in connection with the Delhi riots, and several individuals continue to face legal proceedings in those cases, BJP said. The violence did not appear spontaneous and there were organised groups that initiated it, after which sections of the crowd followed, it alleged.In a hard-hitting statement, BJP functionary Amit Malviya said that the “stone-pelting and orchestrated violence” carried out in the name of students raise serious questions about the intent of the protestors and those backing them.“Even more telling was the conspicuous absence of many of the prominent faces associated with the protest from the march itself, leaving those who had been mobilised at the behest of their political patrons to face the consequences,” he asked. The investigation will establish criminal responsibility, Malviya said.“The real conspirators are often those who plan, encourage and coordinate such violence while carefully keeping themselves out of harm’s way. They move on to the next agitation, leaving those they mobilised to bear the legal consequences.”CJP had been holding protest at Jantar Mantar since last month and has drawn support from opposition parties over its demand for the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan for paper leaks.
Article Summary: CJP had been holding protest at Jantar Mantar since last month and has drawn support from opposition parties over its demand for the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan for paper leaks. The violence did not appear spontaneous and there were organised groups that initiated it, after which sections of the crowd followed.

Article Title: No decision on ethanol blending beyond 20%: Govt
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Amid concerns over E20 petrol, govt Monday said no decision had been taken to increase ethanol blending in petrol beyond 20%.Govt also informed Parliament there was no proposal to make lower-blended fuel or pure petrol available at select retail outlets. It also ruled out any immediate move to introduce ethanol blending in diesel, saying any future decision would depend on evaluation and consultations with stakeholders. "Any move to increase blending in petrol beyond 20% would be based on scientific and technical studies and consultations with automobile manufacturers, oil marketing companies and research institutions," minister of state for petroleum and natural gas Suresh Gopi told Rajya Sabha.He added govt's policy was to progressively transition towards cleaner, technologically superior and environmentally sustainable fuels.India achieved 20% ethanol blending in petrol last year, and E20 is now the standard petrol variant across the country. However, there have been claims of reduced mileage and increased engine wear and tear in older vehicles that are not E20-compliant.Gopi said the transition to E20 had been "phased, calibrated and evidence-based", with blending increasing from 1.5% in 2013-14 to 20% in 2025-26 after scientific evaluation and consultations.Govt also maintained that it had not received any "widespread or substantiated" complaints regarding ethanol-blended petrol affecting vehicle performance and that many such claims circulating on social media were found to be "misleading or unsupported by technical evidence"."Had E20 caused systemic damage to vehicle performance or longevity, it would have been reflected in large-scale warranty claims and service records," the minister said, adding that any reduction in fuel economy in certain E10-designed vehicles was generally limited to about 3-5%.
Article Summary: India achieved 20% ethanol blending in petrol last year, and E20 is now the standard petrol variant across the country. There have been claims of reduced mileage and increased engine wear and tear in older vehicles that are not E20-compliant.

Article Title: Wangchuk continues to refuse IV fluids despite medical advice; low blood sugar, potassium persist
Article Text:
"Sonam Wangchuk being continuously monitored, kidney risk if fasting continues," said Safdarjung Hospital's statement.

NEW DELHI: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk continues to decline intravenous fluids and glucose administration despite repeated medical advice, even as his blood sugar levels remained on the lower side, serum potassium dropped to 3.2 mEq/L and laboratory tests showed persistent pancytopenia, a condition marked by reduced blood cell counts, according to the latest health bulletin issued by VMMC & Safdarjung Hospital on Tuesday morning.The hospital said Wangchuk's vital parameters remain stable, but he continues to have persistent pancytopenia, characterised by anaemia and low leukocyte (white blood cell) counts. These clinical and laboratory findings warrant continued close monitoring through serial investigations, it said.Doctors said Wangchuk is currently receiving oral rehydration therapy (ORS) and oral potassium supplementation. However, he continues to refuse intravenous fluids and glucose administration despite repeated medical advice.According to the bulletin, Wangchuk remains under the close supervision of a multidisciplinary team of experts from VMMC & Safdarjung Hospital and AIIMS, New Delhi. In view of his ongoing mild to moderate dehydration, persistent laboratory abnormalities and the physiological stress associated with prolonged fasting, sustained medical supervision and continuous clinical monitoring remain essential for his recovery and for the timely identification and management of any potential complications, it said.The hospital said all necessary medical care is being provided and Wangchuk's clinical condition remains under close and continuous assessment. Further management will be guided by his clinical progress and serial laboratory investigations.
Article Summary: Sonam Wangchuk's vital parameters remain stable, but he continues to have persistent pancytopenia, characterised by anaemia and low leukocyte (white blood cell) counts. Wangchuk is currently receiving oral rehydration therapy (ORS) and oral potassium supplementation.

Article Title: No let-up even after strict punishment for rape: Supreme Court
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Expressing concern that there has been no let-up in incidents of rape/ gangrape despite punishment for the crimes being made stringent in the aftermath of the horrifying Nirbhaya incident, Supreme Court Monday awarded a 20-year jail term to a rickshaw puller who had raped a woman in 2016 on the pretext of taking her home from New Delhi Railway Station.In its sentencing order, a bench of Justices Sanjay Karol and Augustine George Masih decided to take a sympathetic approach as the convict was only 25 years of age at the time of commission of offence and had no criminal antecedents. Though the trial court and Delhi High Court had sentenced him to spend the rest of his life in jail, apex court modified the quantum of sentence to a 20year jail term.The rickshaw puller was convicted and sentenced in June 2017, and Delhi High Court upheld the order within six months in Nov. While the convict filed an appeal after eight years, apex court decided the case within seven months. On the first date of hearing on Jan 5, apex court had made it clear that it would not interfere with the conviction order and would only examine the quantum of punishment.The bench said that punishing an offender has a trifold purpose, i.e. punitive, deterrent and protective, and a holistic approach should be taken before deciding the quantum of punishment.“The overall objective is to rid society of criminal proclivity. The same is done by ensuring that the sentence imposed is proportional to the offence or, in other words, not overly influenced by the possibility of reformation and instead, striking a judicious balance between the interest of the victim, society and the accused, after following due process of law,” it said.“In the present case, the appellant convict has no criminal antecedents; was only 25 years old when the offence was committed; given the young age there is a possibility of reformation. The State has neither brought on record anything to show that the same would not be possible, nor has it contravened the statement made on behalf of the appellant that in the nearly 10 years (including remission) that he has been convicted, he has maintained good conduct,” the bench said.The court pointed out that such crimes continue to occur regularly, notwithstanding various amendments to the law to deter potential offenders. While these may have had some positive impact, the need to check such crimes with urgency cannot be understated, it said, citing NCRB data, as per which around 30,000 rape cases were reported in India in 2024.
Article Summary: A rickshaw puller was convicted and sentenced in June 2017, and Delhi High Court upheld the order. While the convict filed an appeal after eight years, apex court decided the case within seven months. The court pointed out that such crimes continue to occur regularly, notwithstanding various amendments to the law to deter potential offenders.

Article Title: Global urea prices ease after May spike, fall 40% in June
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The rise in urea prices moderated last month, though it remained 45% higher than in June 2025. As per govt data, international urea prices were $572 per tonne last month, compared to $395 per tonne a year back.In a big relief to govt, urea prices fell to $572 per tonne in June, a 40% reduction compared to prices in May.Industry insiders said this was mainly due to a partial lifting of export restrictions by China and fading fears of prolonged war-induced supply disruptions. “India has a comfortable stock of urea for the current kharif season. We are keeping a close watch on developments in West Asia andits impact on the future supply chain,” a senior industry executive said.As per the fertiliser department’s monthly bulletin of June, prices of diammonium phosphate, potash, phosphoric acid were higher compared to last year. Sulphur saw the maximum rise, being priced at $1,050 per tonne, 265% higher than June 2025 prices. Ammonia prices were also 95% higher than last year.Data shows that domestic urea production in May and June this year was higher compared to the target, offering greater comfort to govt.
Article Summary: Industry insiders said this was mainly due to a partial lifting of export restrictions by China and fading fears of prolonged war-induced supply disruptions. Data shows that domestic urea production in May and June this year was higher compared to the target, offering greater comfort to govt.

Article Title: 1st dengue vaccine gets DCGI nod, set to hit market next year
Article Text:
The latest seven-year data for QDENGA show continued protection against dengue infection (Representative image)

2-dose jab cleared for people aged between 4 & 60

NEW DELHI: India Monday approved its first dengue vaccine, marking a significant milestone in the country’s fight against one of its fastest-growing vector-borne diseases. The Drug Controller General of India (DCGI) granted market authorisation under Form CT-20 of New Drugs and Clinical Trials Rules, 2019, to QDENGA, Takeda’s dengue vaccine, for individuals aged 4 to 60 years.The vaccine requires two doses, given three months apart. It is designed to protect against all four dengue virus serotypes.Unlike an earlier dengue vaccine available internationally, QDENGA can be administered regardless of previous dengue infection and does not require pre-vaccination screening.According to Dr Mahender Nayak, head of Intercontinental Markets at Takeda, the vaccine is expected to be launched in the first half of 2027. The company is working closely with the regulator and other stakeholders ahead of the launch, he added. The pricing has not yet been finalised.The approval in India was supported by a Phase III clinical trial in Indian participants aged 4 to 60 years, which found the vaccine to be safe, well tolerated and immunogenic. According to the company, the vaccine has also received WHO prequalification and is recommended by World Health Organisation for use in dengue-endemic settings without pre-vaccination screening.The approval comes at a time when dengue continues to pose a public health challenge. According to Takeda, reported dengue cases in India have increased 11-fold over the past two decades.A landmark modelling study published in Nature estimated that India accounts for nearly 34% of global dengue burden, the highest for any country.According to the company, more than 1.13 lakh dengue cases were reported in India in 2025. Spread by Aedes mosquitoes, dengue has no specific antiviral treatment and can rapidly progress to severe disease, requiring hospitalisation. “All four dengue virus serotypes circulate in India, contributing to recurrent outbreaks,” said Dr Goh Choo Beng, medical affairs head, Southeast Asia & India Cluster.Takeda said QDENGA has been approved in 43 countries, with more than 32 million doses distributed globally through public and private immunisation programmes. Company said it will work with Indian regulatory authorities and healthcare stakeholders to support the vaccine’s rollout.
Article Summary: India Monday approved its first dengue vaccine, marking a significant milestone in the country’s fight against one of its fastest-growing vector-borne diseases. The Drug Controller General of India (DCGI) granted market authorisation under Form CT-20 of New Drugs and Clinical Trials Rules, 2019, to QDENGA. The vaccine is expected to be launched in the first half of 2027.

Article Title: Supreme Court seeks more exclusive UAPA, NDPS courts to speed up trials
Article Text: NEW DELHI: To address the grievance of accused facing long years of incarceration in UAPA and NDPS cases, SC Monday pushed for the establishment of additional exclusive special courts to speed up trials in NIA-investigated cases and those related to narcotic drugs registered by various police organisations.Additional solicitor general Aishwarya Bhati informed a bench of CJI Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and V Mohana that 14 additional exclusive NIA special courts have been designated in nine states following a series of SC orders and reminders by the Centre. The courts include three each in Gujarat and Jharkhand, two in Maharashtra and one each in J&K, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Manipur, Assam and Punjab.The bench, however, expressed concern over only one NIA court in J&K handling 47 cases and asked the Union govt to consider establishing more special courts for expeditious completion of trials. Delhi is also in the process of designating three exclusive NIA courts to speed up hearings in 50 pending cases.On NDPS cases, the Centre informed the court that exclusive special courts across the country have increased from 77 to 113, but 394 are required based on inputs from states and UTs. West Bengal and Haryana accounted for 13 each among the 36 newly added courts.Govt said states and UTs have committed to establishing the remaining NDPS courts expeditiously in coordination with high courts. It added that infrastructure needs would be met through existing central schemes for judicial infrastructure to avoid duplication of financial assistance.
Article Summary: 14 additional exclusive NIA special courts have been designated in nine states. The courts include three each in Gujarat and Jharkhand, two in Maharashtra and one each in J&K, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Manipur, Assam and Punjab. The bench expressed concern over only one NIA court in J &K handling 47 cases.

Article Title: 'No prima facie case': HC rejects plea of Mamata Banerjee faction to operate TMC accounts worth Rs 440 crore
Article Text:
Calcutta HC

(With PTI inputs)

NEW DELHI: The Calcutta high court on Monday refused interim relief to the Mamata Banerjee-led faction of the Trinamool Congress (TMC), rejecting its plea to operate three party bank accounts frozen by the Enforcement Directorate (ED).Finding no prima facie case in favour of the petitioners, Justice Krishna Rao dismissed the request for interim relief.The court directed the ED to file its affidavit-in-opposition within three weeks, after which the petitioners will have two weeks to submit their reply, if any. The matter will be heard again on August 26.The ED had earlier frozen the three bank accounts, which collectively held Rs 440.42 crore, as part of a money laundering probe linked to an FIR registered by the West Bengal Police over alleged dishonest financial transactions, unlawful collection of funds, and suspected routing of money through certain party accounts.Justice Rao also observed that the ED's freezing order, issued on July 7, was not brought to the notice of a coordinate bench headed by Justice Saugata Bhattacharyya. The court noted that neither the state nor the petitioners informed the bench about the order, as the central agency was not a party to the earlier proceedings.On July 9, Justice Bhattacharyya had appointed a special officer to facilitate the operation of the three accounts by the Mamata Banerjee-led faction of the TMC for day-to-day and legal expenses.The petitioners had approached the court challenging the ED's initiation of an Enforcement Case Information Report (ECIR), the equivalent of an FIR, in the matter.According to ED officials, the agency's preliminary investigation found that around Rs 160 crore was transferred from TMC bank accounts to Carewell Aviation India Pvt. Ltd. and its related entity between April 2023 and June 2026.The company allegedly routed Rs 82.96 crore to another newly incorporated entity during the same period, officials claimed.Investigators found that a "significant" amount was transferred to the entity, of which Rs 112 crore was allegedly used to purchase an Embraer Legacy 600 business jet and an AgustaWestland 109SP helicopter, officials added.
Article Summary: The Calcutta high court on Monday refused interim relief to the Mamata Banerjee- led faction of the Trinamool Congress (TMC) The court rejected its plea to operate three party bank accounts frozen by the Enforcement Directorate. The ED had earlier frozen the three bank accounts, which collectively held Rs 440.42 crore.

Article Title: Kolkata Chai announces tie-up with Bungalow Hospitality Investments
Article Text:
Bungalow Hospitality Investments, co-founded by Michelin-starred chef Vikas Khanna, and New York-born chai brand Kolkata Chai enter into a partnership (Image/PTI)

NEW DELHI: Kolkata Chai, the New York–born chai brand that helped introduce authentic Indian chai to large US audiences, announced a strategic partnership and investment with Bungalow Hospitality Investments, the newly formed venture co‑founded by Michelin‑starred chef Vikas Khanna and restaurateur Jimmy Rizvi.The deal, announced Monday, includes a financial investment in Kolkata Chai and an exclusive menu integration across Bungalow Hospitality Management’s New York City restaurant portfolio. Beginning this summer, Kolkata Chai’s signature Masala Chai will be rolled out at the group’s current and upcoming restaurants, including GupShup and the forthcoming Punjab Meet House. The partnership will also produce a series of limited‑time collaborative menu items this fall.Kolkata Chai was founded in 2019 by brothers Ayan and Ani Sanyal and has grown through a multi‑unit café model and a line of consumer packaged goods (CPG) chai blends. In 2024, actor and comedian Hasan Minhaj joined the company as an investor and business partner, helping expand the brand’s visibility through media and cultural storytelling.Bungalow Hospitality Investments, led by Khanna and Rizvi, focuses on investing in and partnering with emerging food and hospitality brands.Kolkata Chai is the group’s inaugural investment; Khanna cited a personal connection to chai and longstanding admiration for the brand’s craft and authenticity as factors in the decision.“Chef Vikas Khanna is a pioneer for Indian cuisine on a global stage, and having him join the Kolkata Chai family is a genuinely exciting moment for us,” said Hasan Minhaj in a statement. He added that the partnership reflects Kolkata Chai’s role in connecting people across generations and cultures.The announcement comes amid growing mainstream interest in South Asian food and beverage in the United States, driven by restaurant openings and a rising number of South Asian CPG brands. Kolkata Chai and Bungalow Foods, a CPG line co‑founded by Khanna and Rizvi, have both positioned themselves as leaders within that expanding market.
Article Summary: Bungalow Hospitality Investments, co-founded by Michelin-starred chef Vikas Khanna, and New York-born chai brand Kolkata Chai enter into a partnership. The deal, announced Monday, includes a financial investment in KolkATA Chai and an exclusive menu integration across the group’s New York City restaurants.

Article Title: 'Hopefully govt will fix accountability': Sonam Wangchuk to continue his hunger strike
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk on Monday said that that he has decided to continue his hunger strike after seeing the brutality on peaceful protestors marching to the Parliament.In a handwritten note, Wangchuk said that the his hunger strike will continue until Cockroach Janata Party leaders are allowed to meet MPs at the Parliament or he is allowed to meet them at Safdarjung Hospital."Seeing the brutality with which peacefully protesting Youth are being dealt with, I have decided to continue my fast until the youth leaders are allowed to meet the Parliamentarians at the Sansad Bhawan or I am allowed to meet them here at the Hospital," Wangchuk said."Hopefully government will fix accountability of Education minister before that," he added.He also appealed to the government and Delhi Police to allow the protesting students to present their grievances before the Parliament."I am touched & moved by how the youth have held on to peace despite provocation and I appeal to the Govt & Police force to resolve the issue by simply allowing the students to present their grievances before the Parliament today or tomorrow," Wangchuk said."I'm sure the young protesters will show the same patience and tolerance tomorrow as they did today," he added.This comes hours after a delegation of CJP met Union minister JP Nadda and submitted its petition demanding accountability from the government over the NEET-UG paper leak case.According to CJP spokesperson Ashutosh Ranka, the meeting with Nadda was under way, with the Union minister seeking time to consult the government leadership on the party's demands.The demands include the immediate release of activist Sonam Wangchuk, the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan, and Rs 1 crore compensation for the families of NEET aspirants who died by suicide.This comes as thousands tried to march to Parliament as part of the Cockroach Janta Party's protest call to demand the former's resignation over alleged examination irregularities.Outside Parliament, police used tear gas and mild force after demonstrators allegedly attempted to breach barricades and march towards the Parliament complex. Several protesters were detained.Security was tightened across central Delhi, with Rapid Action Force personnel deployed at key locations to prevent any breach. As a precautionary measure, several gates at Metro stations in the area were temporarily closed, affecting commuters.At least seven CJP protesters, including Abhijeet Dipke, were detained near the Reserve Bank of India, close to Parliament, during the party's 'Sansad Chalo' march, PTI reported.
Article Summary: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk on Monday said that that he has decided to continue his fast until Cockroach Janata Party leaders are allowed to meet MPs at the Parliament. Wangchuk also appealed to the government and Delhi Police to allow the protesting students to present their grievances. This comes as thousands tried to march to Parliament as part of the Cockroach Janta Party's protest call.

Article Title: DS Group launches Pallu Protection PPE to shield women farmers from pesticide exposure
Article Text:
.

DS Group has launched an initiative to reduce occupational health risks faced by women farmers from exposure to agricultural pesticides and chemicals, rolling out awareness activities and a culturally tailored protective solution in seven villages of Baghpat district.The programme, part of the company’s FarmHER initiative, is currently being implemented in Pali, Katha, Bandpur, Sunehra, Basi, Mavikala and Sankrodh. It combines community education, preventive health services and distribution of a practical protective product designed for women who commonly use saree pallus or dupattas while working in the fields.As part of the campaign, DS Group introduced a washable nano-fibre filter — marketed as the Pallu Protection Equipment (PPE) filter — that can be stitched into a saree pallu or dupatta to act as a barrier against pesticide particles, dust, harmful chemicals and PM2.5 pollutants. The filters will be made available free of charge through selected tailor shops, pesticide outlets and chemist stores across the seven villages.The company said the intervention was informed by field research which found many women already use pallus or dupattas as informal protection, despite their limited effectiveness. A male version of the product, designed to be integrated into the traditional gamcha, has also been developed.DS Group’s outreach in the villages includes mobile awareness vans, traditional puppet shows, nukkad nataks (street plays), door-to-door education and free health camps. The health camps provide blood pressure and blood sugar screening, basic counselling and distribution of PPE masks. Women participating in the programme also receive mirrors and calendars bearing messages about safer farming practices.“Women farmers play a vital role in India’s agricultural economy, yet awareness of the occupational health risks they face remains limited,” said Rajeev Jain, Senior Vice President, Corporate Marketing, DS Group. “Our field research revealed that the saree pallu and dupatta are often used as informal protective coverings during pesticide-intensive agricultural work, despite offering inadequate protection. This highlights the urgent need for simple, practical and culturally appropriate solutions that women farmers can seamlessly adopt.”DS Group said the initiative aims to bridge the gap between awareness and action by providing accessible, culturally relevant interventions to protect women’s health and encourage safer, more sustainable farming practices as part of its broader FarmHER programme.
Article Summary: DS Group has launched an initiative to reduce occupational health risks faced by women farmers from exposure to agricultural pesticides and chemicals. The programme, part of the company’s FarmHER initiative, is currently being implemented in Pali, Katha, Bandpur, Sunehra, Basi, Mavikala and Sankrodh.

Article Title: 'It destroys academic freedom': TMC on VBSA bill as JPC defers report
Article Text:
.

NEW DELHI: With the JPC deciding to defer the report on the Viksit Bharat Shiksha Adhisthan bill, Trinamool Congress MP Sagarika Ghose said the opposition forced the govt to put a hold on the controversial legislation that would destroy the education sector.Ghose said, “The shelving of the bill is a big win for the opposition. Trinamool Congress pushed back against the VBSA bill which is anti-student and anti-federalism.”TMC sources said the bill exceeds the constitutional parameters while making higher education the preserve of the rich. “It attacks federalism, creates a massive centralized structure and rule by New Delhi. It destroys academic freedom and constitutes a narrow interpretation of Bharatiya Knowledge Systems based on only one religion.”A senior TMC leader claimed the govt intends to demolish the UGC, in the absence of which the universities will take loans and repayment of the loans will lead to hiking of fees. “How will poor students afford education,” she asked.The bill was strongly opposed by Congress, DMK, Shiv Sena while, it is reported, govt ally TDP too had reservations over it.
Article Summary: The JPC decided to defer the report on the Viksit Bharat Shiksha Adhisthan bill. Trinamool Congress MP Sagarika Ghose said the opposition forced the govt to put a hold on the controversial legislation that would destroy the education sector.

Article Title: Ram Temple donation row: Trust deploys new cash-counting team, introduces two-shift system under SBI supervision
Article Text:
Ram Temple in Ayodhya

NEW DELHI: The Ram Temple trust in Ayodhya has deployed a new team to count cash offerings from donation boxes and introduced a two-shift system under the supervision of the State Bank of India (SBI), even as an investigation continues into allegations of donation theft.The responsibility for counting the money collected from donation boxes installed inside the Ram Temple premises lies with the SBI.The exercise began on Sunday after the completion of a week-long trial, following which the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, which manages the temple, formally handed over the cash-counting process to SBI, PTI reported, citing sources.According to temple sources, 10 to 12 personnel have been assigned to each shift. The first shift runs from 8 am to 2 pm, while the second operates from 2 pm to 8 pm.The trust has appointed Shobhnath Mishra as the new in-charge of cash counting after his predecessor, Subhash Shrivastava, was sent to jail in connection with the alleged donation theft case.To strengthen monitoring, the trust has appointed four supervisors, including a former serviceman, to oversee the counting process.Trust security in-charge and former Army officer Captain KK Tiwari has been designated the senior in-charge and will be responsible for submitting daily reports on the exercise.Former defence personnel SP Dubey and Sheshmani Tiwari have also been appointed as trust representatives to supervise the cash-counting process.The alleged embezzlement of Ram Temple donations came to light in the first week of June, following which the Uttar Pradesh government constituted a Special Investigation Team (SIT) on June 13 to investigate the matter.After the SIT submitted its preliminary report on June 23, an FIR was registered on June 25. Eight accused—Avinash Shukla, Anukalp Mishra, Lavkush Mishra, Manish Kumar Yadav, Karunesh Pandey, Ramashankar Mishra, Subhash Srivastava and Ramashankar Yadav alias Tinnu Yadav—were arrested.During the investigation, police recovered cash from several accused, with the highest recovery of Rs 20.39 lakh from Avinash Shukla. Authorities also seized gold, silver, foreign currency and a donation box marked "Ramrajya Kosh."
Article Summary: The Ram Temple trust in Ayodhya has deployed a new team to count cash offerings from donation boxes. The two-shift system is under the supervision of the State Bank of India. The trust has appointed four supervisors, including a former serviceman, to oversee the counting process.

Article Title: Delhi HC seeks comprehensive medical record on Sonam Wangchuk's condition
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The Delhi high court on Monday sought a comprehensive record of climate activist Sonam Wangchuk 's medical condition and directed the authorities to place all relevant health documents before it.A division bench of Chief Justice D K Upadhyaya and Justice Tejas Karia asked the authorities to submit all pathological reports prepared by Safdarjung Hospital, AIIMS and the private laboratory, along with every health bulletin issued on Wangchuk's condition."The report should include today's sample analysis", said the bench said.It also directed the appellant to file reports of tests conducted through the private laboratory.The court further directed the doctor heading the medical team treating Wangchuk at Safdarjung Hospital, AIIMS Director, and Dr Akshay, In-charge of Emergency Medicine at AIIMS, to remain present before it on Tuesday. It also requested the doctor engaged by Wangchuk's family to appear and assist the court.The court said that it would take a call on Tuesday on whether to allow Angmo's plea to shift Wangchuk to a private facility.The directions came while hearing an appeal filed by Wangchuk's wife against a single judge's refusal to interfere with the Centre's decision to shift the activist to Safdarjung Hospital.On Sunday, the single judge had upheld the government's decision, observing that shifting Wangchuk to the hospital was not arbitrary in view of his deteriorating health after weeks of fasting.The court had issued notice to the Centre and directed it to file a status report within three days."Since the government has taken the decision to shift him to the hospital on account of his medical condition, this court does not find the same to be an arbitrary action," the court had observed."Since doctors at Safdarjung are closely monitoring his health and have only given oral things as per his consent, it can't be said any force is being used against him," it added.
Article Summary: Delhi high court seeks a comprehensive record of climate activist Sonam Wangchuk's medical condition. The court directed the authorities to place all relevant health documents before it. The directions came while hearing an appeal filed by Wangchuk's wife against a single judge's refusal to interfere with the Centre's decision to shift the activist to Safdarjung Hospital.

Article Title: Ram temple donation 'theft' row: Simple case of crime, don't politicise it, says SC
Article Text:
Ram temple donation 'theft' row: Simple case of crime, don't politicise it, says SC

NEW DELHI: The Supreme Court on Monday said the alleged Ram temple donation embezzlement case should not be politicised, observing that it was 'a simple case of commission of crime' and that the court's role was only to ensure a proper investigation into the matter.The apex court made the remarks while hearing petitions seeking a court-monitored probe into the alleged embezzlement of donations at the Ram temple in Ayodhya, including demands for a CBI investigation, a forensic audit and an audit by the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust's accounts.The bench was headed by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and V Mohana.During the hearing, the bench said, "It is a simple case of commission of crime. We are only here to ensure a proper investigation. Courts are not a place for politics." The court also asked the parties not to politicise the issue.The Supreme Court asked Solicitor General Tushar Mehta to take instructions on whether a Special Investigation Team (SIT) could be set up to probe the alleged embezzlement of donations at the Ram temple.Responding to the court, the solicitor general said an SIT had already been constituted and that the Uttar Pradesh Police was investigating the case.The matter has been posted for further hearing on July 27, when the Uttar Pradesh government will apprise the court about the progress of the SIT probe.The investigation gathered pace after the SIT submitted an interim report that led to the registration of an FIR, the arrest of eight accused and the recovery of nearly Rs 80 lakh allegedly siphoned off from temple donations. The probe also triggered the resignations of former Trust general secretary Champat Rai and former trustee Anil Mishra.According to sources, the SIT has sought additional time to examine financial records, administrative procedures and the alleged irregularities before submitting its final report. The report is also expected to recommend reforms in the temple's donation collection, counting and administrative systems.The petitions before the Supreme Court seek a court-monitored CBI investigation, a forensic audit and a CAG audit of the Trust's finances, while questioning the adequacy of the ongoing state probe. The Trust is scheduled to meet in Ayodhya on July 22 to discuss the SIT's findings and consider corrective measures based on its recommendations.
Article Summary: The Supreme Court made the remarks while hearing petitions seeking a court- monitored probe into the alleged embezzlement of donations at the Ram temple. The court also asked the parties not to politicise the issue. The matter has been posted for further hearing on July 27, when the Uttar Pradesh government will apprise the court about the progress of the SIT probe.

Article Title: Shambhu border sealed as farmers head to Delhi to oppose India-US trade deal
Article Text:
Farmers marched towards Delhi to protest the proposed Indo-US trade agreement. Police barricaded the Shambhu border, preventing many from reaching their destination.

By: Neel Kamal

BATHINDA: A large number of farmers from Punjab, who were on their way to Delhi to hold a Kisan Mahapanchayat at Kisan Ghat, were stopped at Shambhu on the border of Punjab and Haryana.The Haryana Police put up barricades to stop farmers from moving ahead to raise voice against proposed Indo-US trade ageeement.The barricading was done as was done in February 2024 when farmers had given call for Delhi Chalo-2 demanding legal right of minimum support price on all crops. Then farmers kept on sitting for over a year at Shambhu and Khanauri borders.The farmers from Punjab upon being stopped at Shambhu decided to hold protest there and accused both Punjab and Haryana governments of stopping farmers from reaching Delhi.BKU Shaheed Bhagat Singh functionary from Ambala Tezveer Singh said that Haryana police put up barricades on the territory of Punjab and the Punjab government not stopped them from stopping farmers in Punjab.The farmers from Haryana though headed towards Delhi from Ambala, Kurukshetra and other places.Farmers from many states including Punjab, Haryana and Himachal Pradesh Monday had left for Delhi on Monday to participate in Kisan Mahapanchayat under the banner of Desh Bachao Morcha on Tuesday, demanding the cancellation of the proposed Indo–US Free Trade Agreement (FTA).Farmer organization Kisan Mazdoor Sangharsh Committee (KMSC) functionary Sarwan Singh Pandher said that the convoys of KMSC, Kisan Mazdoor Morcha (Punjab Chapter), Azaad Kisan Morcha (AKM), BKU Ekta Sangharsh, headed to Delhi from different parts of Punjab.Pandher stated that in the India–US joint statement issued in February 2026, both countries agreed to work towards negotiating the first tranche of a mutually beneficial, multi -sector bilateral trade agreement (BTA) by fall 2026.This indicates that the proposed agreement will not be limited to a few commodities but may encompass agriculture, dairy, industry, digital trade, e-commerce, investment, government procurement, intellectual property rights, and the services sector.He further said that previous US trade documents and annual trade reports have consistently sought greater market access in India's agriculture and dairy sectors.These documents have called for reducing tariffs on products such as soybeans, corn (maize), cotton, ethanol, apples, almonds, and other agricultural commodities, while also seeking wider access for the US dairy products, poultry, fruits, fisheries products, coconuts, and other commodities in the Indian market.Farmer functionaries pointed out that while the United States provides substantial subsidies to its farmers, the overwhelming majority of Indian farmers are small and marginal landholders.If low-cost American agricultural and dairy products enter the Indian market on a large scale, they could seriously threaten the livelihoods of Indian farmers, dairy producers, small traders, and micro and small enterprises.They further stated that India's dairy sector supports the livelihoods of nearly 80 million rural families.Any adverse impact on this sector could have far-reaching consequences for the rural economy and employment. Similarly, the seed industry, food processing sector, small businesses, and agriculture -based industries may also face significant negative effects.The Desh Bachao Morcha demanded that the Central Government make all documents related to the proposed India–US Trade Agreement public and refrain from signing any agreement without holding comprehensive consultations with farmers, workers, dairy producers, small traders, and other stakeholders.
Article Summary: Police barricaded the Shambhu border, preventing many from reaching their destination. The barricading was done as was done in February 2024 when farmers had given call for Delhi Chalo-2 demanding legal right of minimum support price on all crops. If low-cost American agricultural and dairy products enter the Indian market on a large scale, they could threaten the livelihoods of Indian farmers.

Article Title: Delhi traffic police issues advisory ahead of farmers' protest at Kisan Ghat; check affected routes
Article Text:
Delhi Police issues traffic advisory as diversions likely near Kisan Ghat

Farmer protest at Kisan Ghat today; Delhi traffic police issues advisory.

General instruction

NEW DELHI: Traffic movement is likely to be affected in parts of central Delhi on Tuesday as the Delhi Traffic Police has issued an advisory ahead of a demonstration scheduled at Kisan Ghat from 8.30am.Commuters have been warned of possible diversions and congestion on several key roads.According to the advisory, diversions may be enforced on a need basis to ensure smooth traffic management and public safety.Traffic is likely to be diverted from Delhi Gate, Rajghat Crossing, Guru Nanak Chowk, Shantivan Chowk, Rajghat DTC Depot and the IP Flyover.Motorists have been advised to avoid Satyagrah Marg, Velodrome Road, Rajghat DTC Depot Road, Rajghat DTC Bypass Road and surrounding stretches, where traffic movement is expected to be affected due to the gathering.The Traffic Police has urged commuters travelling to the airport, railway stations and adjoining areas to plan their journeys in advance and use navigation applications for real-time traffic updates.People have also been advised to follow the directions of traffic personnel deployed on the ground, park vehicles only at designated parking areas and avoid roadside parking.The advisory said commuters may face inconvenience during the period of restrictions and appealed to the public to cooperate with traffic personnel and adhere to traffic rules to ensure hassle-free movement across the city.
Article Summary: Commuters have been warned of possible diversions and congestion on several key roads. Traffic is likely to be diverted from Delhi Gate, Rajghat Crossing, Guru Nanak Chowk, Shantivan Chowk and the IP Flyover. Motorists have been advised to avoid Satyagrah Marg, Velodrome Road, Rajgat DTC Depot Road and surrounding stretches, where traffic movement is expected to be affected.

Article Title: Despite 35 minute delay Vikram-1 keeps date with Indian space history with Mission Aagaman success
Article Text: SRIHARIKOTA: After some anxious moments when the automatic launch sequence of Vikram-1 was halted midway at just five minutes before lift-off, Skyroot Aerospace finally created history after it successfully placed India’s first privately developed orbital-class rocket into space.The seven-storey tall Vikram-1 rocket, with a lift-off weight of 45,000 kg, blasted off at 12.05 pm, a good 35 minutes behind the originally scheduled time, from the first launch pad at the Satish Dhawan Space Centre-Sriharikota Range on its maiden orbital test flight, Mission Aagaman.The vehicle completed its planned flight sequence and placed its payloads into the targeted low Earth orbit, validating key systems developed by the Hyderabad-based startup.Prime Minister Narendra Modi spoke personally with the Skyroot founders Pawan Kumar Chandana and Naga Bharath Daka on phone and congratulated the duo on the successful completion of Mission Aagaman.The PM, who sent a postcard into space abroad Vikram-1 with Vande Mataram written on it, pointed out to the Skyroot team that the feat was also momentous coming as it does when Vande Mataram is commemorating 150 years.The feat marks a major milestone for the country’s commercial launch sector and opens a new chapter at Sriharikota 46 years after ISRO’s first successfully launched India’s first launch vehicle SLV-3 into orbit.The launch came exactly 46 years after ISRO’s SLV-3E2 placed the Rohini satellite RS-1 into orbit on July 18, 1980, making India the sixth nation capable of launching satellites independently. Like SLV-3, Vikram-1 stands 22 metres tall, but its success represents a different leap: the arrival of private Indian industry in orbital launch operations.Built with an all-carbon composite structure, Vikram-1 uses in-house propulsion systems, including high-thrust solid-fuel stages and 3D-printed engines. The mission targeted a 450 km orbit at an inclination of 60 degrees and lasted just under 18 minutes.The rocket carried a mix of demonstration and symbolic payloads, including Skyroot’s SCOPE payload, SOLARAS, a 1U CubeSat from Karnataka-based Grahaa Space, Embrace, an in-orbit robotic arm demonstration from Telangana-based Cosmoserve Space, and payloads from Germany’s DCubed GmbH.It also carried a handwritten postcard from Prime Minister Narendra Modi bearing the words “Vande Mataram”, along with hundreds of letters from Skyroot’s team and supporters.In attendance at the historic launch were astronaut Shubhanshu Shukla, Andhra Pradesh IT minister Nara Lokesh, with his son in tow, in addition to ISRO chairman V Narayanan, IN-SPACe chairman Pawan Goenka and former ISRO director S Somanath.Founded in 2018 by former ISRO scientists Chandana and Daka, Skyroot had earlier made history on Nov 18, 2022 when Vikram-S became the first privately built Indian rocket to reach space on a suborbital flight. With Aagaman, the company has now moved from demonstration to orbital capability.
Article Summary: Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched India's first privately developed orbital-class rocket into space. The seven-storey tall Vikram-1 rocket blasted off at 12.05 pm, a good 35 minutes behind the originally scheduled time. Prime Minister Narendra Modi spoke personally with the Skyroot founders Pawan Kumar Chandana and Naga Bharath Daka on phone.

Article Title: Saudi Arabia launches new one-year, multiple-entry Umrah visa with 90-day stay
Article Text:
Saudi Arabia has launched a new 90-day multiple entry Umrah visa.

Saudi Arabia has launched a new multiple-entry Umrah visa valid for one year, allowing pilgrims to enter the Kingdom several times within a year while staying for a combined total of up to 90 days, Saudi Press Agency reported.The Ministry of Hajj and Umrah announced the new visa as part of efforts to make religious travel more flexible and improve services for pilgrims in line with Saudi Vision 2030 and the Pilgrim Experience Program.Under the new rules, visa holders can make multiple visits during the one-year validity period. However, the total duration of all stays cannot exceed 90 days.The ministry said pilgrims must purchase a service package through an approved provider on the Nusuk platform before each visit. The package duration must not exceed the number of days remaining on the visa.Pilgrims must also obtain an Umrah permit through the Nusuk application before arriving in Saudi Arabia.The ministry explained that the visa will automatically deactivate when a pilgrim leaves the Kingdom and reactivate only after the traveller meets the required regulations for a subsequent visit.The multiple-entry visa will remain unavailable during the Hajj season. It will not be activated from the first day of Dhu Al-Qi'dah until the 13th day of Dhu Al-Hijjah.(This is a top Google Trends topic.)
Article Summary: Saudi Arabia has launched a new 90-day multiple entry Umrah visa. visa holders can make multiple visits during the one-year validity period. The visa will automatically deactivate when a pilgrim leaves the Kingdom and reactivate only after the traveller meets the required regulations for a subsequent visit.

Article Title: Boom at the front, bust at home: Russia’s war economy is holding but its people are cracking
Article Text:
Are Russians really going bankrupt in record numbers?

Has the war economy masked deeper problems?

Why have high interest rates become a problem?

Are Russian banks under pressure?

What are Russia's biggest banks saying?

Have sanctions finally started to bite?

How has the war reshaped Russia's economy?

Is the war creating hidden economic costs?

Does rising bankruptcy mean Russia is heading for collapse?

By 2024, Russia appeared to have defied expectations. Despite unprecedented Western sanctions, the economy grew by 4.9%, unemployment fell to record lows, and factories linked to the defence sector ran around the clock. President Vladimir Putin pointed to these figures as proof that Russia had adapted to wartime conditions.But beneath the headline numbers another story has quietly emerged: more Russians are declaring bankruptcy, banks are becoming increasingly cautious, borrowing costs remain among the highest in two decades, and some of the country's largest lenders warn of deteriorating loan quality.So, is Russia's economy finally beginning to crack? Or are rising bankruptcies simply the inevitable side effect of an economy adjusting after years of extraordinary wartime spending?As the war in Ukraine grinds into 2026, more than four years since Moscow's full-scale invasion in February 2022, that question has grown harder to dodge. The answer lies somewhere in between. Official data suggests that while Russia is far from economic collapse, the burden of sustaining its war economy is increasingly shifting onto households and the financial system.Short answer: yes—but that does not mean Russia itself is collapsing.A European intelligence assessment obtained by Reuters and prepared for EU policymakers in June 2026 said more than 500,000 Russians declared personal bankruptcy in 2025, almost one-third higher than the previous year.The report also said more than 13 million Russians simultaneously held at least three loans, reflecting rising household indebtedness.Personal bankruptcy in Russia is a legal process that allows individuals overwhelmed by debt to restructure or write off liabilities. A rise in bankruptcies therefore signals growing financial stress among households, not the insolvency of the state. The Bank of Russia has acknowledged rising household debt but rejects suggestions that these figures indicate a systemic banking crisis.Russia performed far better than many Western analysts expected after the 2022 invasion. According to the IMF's World Economic Outlook (July 2026), Russia's economy expanded by 4.9% in 2024, outperforming many major economies despite sweeping sanctions. That growth was largely fuelled by record government spending on the military and defence-industrial production, which kept factories operating and supported strong labour demand. This wartime mobilisation pushed unemployment to exceptionally low levels. The IMF projects unemployment at 2.4% in 2026 — among the lowest for major economies.But that exceptional growth faded quickly. The IMF now projects growth of only 1.0% in 2025 and 1.1% in 2026–27, suggesting the wartime boom is giving way to a prolonged period of slow growth rather than a sharp recession. The World Bank reached a similar conclusion, citing weaker fiscal stimulus, high interest rates and structural constraints that slowed growth after 2024 government spending began to moderate.Anupam Manur, professor of economics at the Takshashila Institution, said Russia's economy "is still not collapsing, but the financial sector is under stress." He argued the government had lowered lending standards to keep the economy and the war machine running, pushing banks to lend to riskier borrowers — a factor now showing up as financial strain and rising bankruptcies.One major pressure on households and businesses is borrowing costs. To combat inflation, the Bank of Russia raised its key rate to 21% in 2024, the highest since the early 2000s. Although the central bank began easing in 2026, lowering the rate to 14.25% in June, borrowing costs remain far above pre-war levels.The Bank of Russia said inflation slowed but that inflation expectations remained elevated, lending had accelerated again, and persistent fiscal deficits could require a tighter policy than previously expected. High rates have made mortgages, consumer loans and corporate borrowing substantially more expensive. The World Bank notes that high borrowing costs and increased taxes sharply curtailed credit growth, while investment contracted by more than 3.6% in 2025 as businesses postponed expansion.Here opinions diverge. The European intelligence report warns that banks are increasingly exposed after years of subsidised lending to defence companies, regional projects and mortgage borrowers. It estimates around 10% of corporate loans could already be doubtful, while some major banks reported retail non-performing loan ratios of up to 15% in 2025. The report argues that state-backed lending programmes and repeated restructurings have masked the true quality of bank assets, creating an "explosive" vulnerability if another shock hits.The Bank of Russia strongly disputes this. Deputy Governor Filipp Gabunia said in June 2026 that the central bank "does not see signs of a banking crisis", noting banks' capital buffers are at their highest in three years and corporate bad loans remained broadly stable at around 4%.The truth likely sits between these positions. There is no clear evidence of an imminent banking collapse, but rising credit risks are apparent if growth continues to weaken.Statements from major lenders provide direct evidence of strain. On July 1, 2026, Sberbank said it would lower its corporate lending growth forecast because of borrowers' worsening financial condition. CFO Taras Skvortsov said corporate bad debts were rising amid high rates and slowing growth. VTB announced plans to increase loan-loss provisions to prepare for possible defaults. Such admissions matter because these institutions have direct exposure across the economy.The intelligence report added that banks had been pushed into subsidised lending for defence, homebuyers and regional projects, with government support and restructurings masking vulnerabilities.Anupam Manur said the central bank's claims of resilience and the surge in bankruptcies reflect different aspects of the economy: the former measures banking solvency, the latter household distress. "Both signals can be true," he said. He advised investors to watch deposit rates, fiscal deficits, the rouble, and inflation expectations for signs of a broader crisis.Sanctions have not produced the sudden collapse many predicted in 2022; instead, their effects have accumulated. The World Bank cites lower oil and gas revenues, deeper discounts on Russian crude, higher taxes and tighter restrictions as contributors to slowing growth. The EU in 2026 proposed a 21st sanctions package targeting almost 90 additional banks, cryptocurrency platforms and oil trading networks, arguing Russia increasingly relies on smaller financial institutions to bypass restrictions.IMF research shows large defence spending booms can boost activity short term, particularly in defence industries, supporting employment and output. But they also create medium-term trade-offs: fiscal deficits typically worsen, public debt rises, and inflationary pressures increase. In Russia's case, wartime expenditure cushioned the immediate shock from sanctions but coincided with high interest rates, slowing private investment, and growing household pressure — illustrating that macro resilience can coexist with micro-level stress.Yes. Repeated Ukrainian drone attacks on oil refineries and disruptions in maritime routes prompted Russia to impose a temporary diesel export ban in July 2026 to stabilise domestic fuel supplies after shortages and rising prices. The Bank of Russia warned such disruptions could raise inflationary risks, and Reuters reported long fuel queues in several regions.Labour shortages persist. Although unemployment is historically low, wage growth has outpaced productivity, increasing inflationary pressure and raising costs for businesses outside the defence sector. Manur said Russia's wartime resilience has largely come at households' expense: high policy rates, elevated inflation eroding real incomes, and a consumer credit boom that pulled less creditworthy borrowers into unsecured debt.Not necessarily. Manur described the situation as financial stress, not economic collapse. The IMF, World Bank and Bank of Russia agree the economy is slowing but none sees imminent collapse. The IMF expects growth to stabilise near 1.1%, while the Bank of Russia believes inflation will move back toward its 4% target over the medium term. The central bank continues to describe the banking system as well-capitalised and resilient.However, mounting pressure on households is clear: higher borrowing costs, slowing wage growth outside defence industries, elevated household debt and weaker private investment all suggest ordinary Russians are increasingly bearing the economic costs of a prolonged war.
Article Summary: Russia performed far better than many Western analysts expected after the 2022 invasion. The IMF now projects growth of only 1.0% in 2025 and 1.1% in 2026–27. The Bank of Russia has acknowledged rising household debt but rejects suggestions that these figures indicate a systemic banking crisis.

Article Title: Intel's fired CEO Pat Gelsinger says he has a hunch when the chipmaker's downfall began: I think one of the fundamental things is …
Article Text:
"I think one of the fundamental things is, and as you look at the great technology companies today, they're deeply technical."

"was the first technical leader in essentially 15 years associated with it."

"When you're making these hardcore technical decisions that affect billions of dollars, you don't do that through a spreadsheet.

That's a lousy investment unless the technology trends make it the right investment."

Intel's former CEO Pat Gelsinger points to leadership and capital allocation



"What I wouldn't have done for another hundred billion dollars on the balance sheet?"

"When you turn off a fab, it doesn't come back on for 90 days. The economic impact of a brownout of Taiwan is greater than the Great Depression in the world."

Intel's former CEO Pat Gelsinger says he believes the chipmaker's decline began years before he returned to lead the company in 2021. Speaking during an interview on the All-In Podcast recorded at the Raise Summit in Paris, Gelsinger said,Gelsinger argued that Intel had moved away from technical leadership before his return, saying heHe added that major technology investment decisions should not be driven primarily by financial analysis, saying,Gelsinger said Intel's previous leadership teams lacked the technical background to guide the company through major technology transitions.Another former Intel CEO, Paul Otellini, who led the company from 2005 to 2013, was the first non-engineer to head Intel. He was succeeded by Brian Krzanich, who worked his way up through Intel's manufacturing operations, and later by Bob Swan, whose background was in finance, including leadership roles at eBay and HP Enterprise Services.Gelsinger also criticised the company's capital allocation before he returned. According to Intel's financial filings, the company returned about $79 billion to shareholders through dividends and stock buybacks between 2015 and 2020.Reflecting on that spending, Gelsinger said,These comments from Gelsinger come as Intel rebuilds its position. The company lost market share over the years as competitors, including TSMC, Samsung, AMD and Nvidia, expanded their positions in the semiconductor industry.The company's shares have risen over the past year following the US government's decision under President Donald Trump to acquire an approximately 10% stake in Intel. Nvidia also disclosed a deal to purchase more than $5 billion worth of Intel shares, giving it an approximately 4% stake in the company.During the interview, Gelsinger also discussed geopolitical risks surrounding semiconductor manufacturing in Taiwan. Referring to the potential impact of disruptions to chip production, he said,
Article Summary: Intel's former CEO Pat Gelsinger says he believes the chipmaker's decline began years before he returned to lead the company in 2021. He said Intel's previous leadership teams lacked the technical background to guide the company through major technology transitions. He also criticised the company's capital allocation before he return.

Article Title: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis to everyone fearing AI replacing them: Look at what we humans have built around us, it is ...
Article Text:
Demis Hassabis said ‘We don’t have long to prepare’

A divided AI community

Demis Hassabis suggests US-lead watchdog for AI companies across the world

Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis has once again predicted that artificial intelligence is close at hand, telling an audience at Stanford Graduate School of Business that AGI could arrive around 2030, plus or minus a year. He called it an “enormous transformative technology” that will usher in “a new human era.” Hassabis also added that a decade from now, society will look back and realise it was “standing in the foothills of the singularity” — the moment when AI surpasses human intelligence and begins improving itself autonomously.Hassabis has warned that society must prepare for the profound implications of AGI. With AI already automating daily tasks and coding work, fears of job displacement are rising. AGI — capable of reasoning, decision‑making and adaptation — could take on far more complex responsibilities.But Hassabis also emphasized that certain human qualities will become even more valuable. In an interview with The Rundown AI’s Rowan Cheung, he said people with “taste, design sensibility, original thinking” and the ability to synthesize ideas across disciplines will be in a strong position over the next five years.“Look at what we built around us”To those worried about being replaced, Hassabis offered a reminder of human ingenuity:“We’re general intelligences ourselves, don’t forget. Look at what we built around us — it’s incredible — with our hunter‑gatherer brains.
